Florida voters sent a decisive message when 65% voted to expand voting rights for felons who have already served their time and now seek to meaningfully contribute to society. But Republicans in the Florida State House want to force through a new bill that's being compared to the poll tax -- **harkening back the dangerous and racist policies enacted to hurt voters after the Civil War.** [**Will you add your name to tell Florida Republicans that we won 't stand for this egregious attack on voting rights and our democracy? >>**](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d0797/72412298/471933a6/6100ffee/1440533881/VEsF/p/eyJlM3NnY21WamFYQnBaVzUwTG1WdFlXbHNJSDE5IjoiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iLCJlM3NnY21WamFYQnBaVzUwTG5wcGNDQjlmUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/protect-voting-rights?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=3+-+Will+you+add+your+name+to+tell+Florida+R&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_190403_ProtectVotingRights_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_EN_190403_ProtectVotingRights_U1_X1") | | | [SIGN TODAY >>](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d0797/72412298/471933a6/6100ffd1/1440533881/VEsC/p/eyJlM3NnY21WamFYQnBaVzUwTG1WdFlXbHNJSDE5IjoiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iLCJlM3NnY21WamFYQnBaVzUwTG5wcGNDQjlmUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/protect-voting-rights?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=4+-+SIGN+TODAY+gtgt&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_190403_ProtectVotingRights_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_EN_190403_ProtectVotingRights_U1_X1")
---
It's legally and morally important that we stand up against blatantly discriminatory policies that do nothing but harm and stigmatize those seeking a second chance at life.
Point blank: if someone who is convicted of a crime has successfully completed their sentence, they should be allowed to cast a ballot.
The GOP has made it their mission to dismantle voting rights for Americans all over the country -- including critical battleground states like Florida. We cannot let them get away with this:
[**Hurry Peter -- Will you take less than a minute to add your name to our petition? >>**](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d0797/72412298/471933a6/6100ffd0/1440533881/VEsD/p/eyJlM3NnY21WamFYQnBaVzUwTG1WdFlXbHNJSDE5IjoiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iLCJlM3NnY21WamFYQnBaVzUwTG5wcGNDQjlmUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/protect-voting-rights?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=5+-+Hurry+firstname++Will+you+take+less+than&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_190403_ProtectVotingRights_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_EN_190403_ProtectVotingRights_U1_X1")

subject: Bias Crimes: What They're Saying

body:
Yesterday, Governor Eric Holcomb made history by signing Indiana's first ever bias crimes law. With his signature -- and the the work of member of... | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/indiana/bias-crimes-what-theyre-saying?e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| |
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=47595543e1&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| Yesterday, Governor Eric Holcomb made history by signing Indiana's first ever bias crimes law.

This new law allows judges to enhance sentences on criminals who commit crimes because of an aggravating bias that targets a group -- better protecting and defending Hoosiers who are victims of senseless crimes.

With his leadership -- and the the support of members of the Indiana General Assembly led by House Speaker Brian Bosma and Senate President Pro Tem Rod Bray -- Indiana is now OFF the list of states without a bias crimes law and ON the map of states that have one!
_**Check out what Hoosiers are saying about Indiana's new bias crimes law:**_

**Governor Eric Holcomb:** _“Months ago, I decided to make protecting Hoosiers against bias crimes a key part of my 2019 legislative agenda. Today, finally, Senate Bill 198 accomplished that goal and I will sign it into law as soon as it gets to my desk. _

_“Those targeted for crimes because of color, creed, disability, national origin, race, religion or sexual orientation are protected. But this new law goes further. It also will cover bias crimes committed because of other traits and characteristics, such as gender, or gender identity, or sex, or age, and other commonly targeted groups._

_“Our new law will allow judges to enhance sentences based on listed and non-listed categories. Criminals who attempt to instill fear by attacking others based, for example, on who someone loves, who they are, how they identify, how they pray, should know their sentences can, and I believe should, be enhanced to the fullest extent of the law.”_

---
|
---
| **Lt. Governor Suzanne Crouch:** _"By signing this enrolled act into law, we are widening the umbrella of judicial coverage for more Hoosiers. I stand with Governor Holcomb in the calling of protecting all individuals regardless of their color, creed, disability, national origin, race, religion or sexual orientation and allowing a judge to consider at sentencing whether a crime was committed with bias or because of a victim's real or perceived characteristic, trait, belief, association or other attribute. By adding these descriptions, we are removing Indiana from the list of states that do not have this clarification in statute. I look forward to addressing the needs of our communities so we can continue to take Indiana and the Hoosiers that call it home to the Next Level."_

---
|
---
| **Speaker Brian Bosma:** _“This bill provides an excellent solution and it covers all Hoosiers equally. You cannot name a characteristic or trait not covered under this bill. We believe this is the most comprehensive and inclusive bias crimes statute in the nation. The reference to our current bias crime definition meets or exceeds that of 21 other states’ bias crime statutes, and all of those states are off the list of states without a bias crimes law. There’s no reasonable assertion as to why this all-inclusive measure doesn’t take Indiana off the list.”_

---
|
---
| **Senate President Pro Tem Rodric Bray:** _“By referencing a specific list of characteristics, Senate Bill 198 provides guidance for judges, while still giving them the flexibility to increase the sentence for any bias-motivated crime as they see fit. I’m pleased to see this bill advance to the governor’s desk. It’s truly inclusive and will provide protections for all Hoosiers.” _

---
|
---
| **Former Indiana Supreme Court Justice Frank Sullivan, Jr.:** _“The language of the bill is clear that acts of bias that are specifically referred to are not exclusive: Gender and gender identity, as well as age, citizenship, martial status, etc. are authorized to be used as an aggravating circumstance in determining a sentence every bit as much as race or religion or sexual identity. Just because a characteristic or trait is not specifically listed does not prevent it from being used to impose a harsher sentence_. _That was the conclusion reach by the Indiana Supreme Court in its unanimous Witmer v. State decision, a case in which I participated while a justice on the court.

"...To be clear: If a person thinks that under this law he or she can commit a crime with bias due to a victim's gender or gender identity without risk of a harsher sentence, that person is wrong."_

---
|
---
| **Kevin Brinegar, Indiana Chamber of Commerce President:** _“The bias crimes legislation heading to the Governor is a big step in the right direction. Though the list is not as comprehensive as we had advocated for, what the Legislature has passed is still a meaningful hate crimes bill. It is more inclusive than some states’ laws and on par with others. Not to mention, it’s far better than having no law at all. _

_“...No one should want to perpetuate a false perception about our state being unwelcoming; that only exacerbates our workforce challenges.”_

---
|
---
| **Micah Clark, American Family Association of Indiana Executive Director:** _“Indiana avoided a California style, left-wing, hate crime bill based on identity politics that could have threatened freedom of speech or provided justice for only a select few. What the legislature passed keeps this issue in the hands of judges, giving them the broadest possible means of sentencing for these crimes.”_

---
|
---
| **State Senator Jim Merritt, Indianapolis Mayoral Candidate:** _“Today’s passage of the bias-crimes bill is a substantial first step forward for Indiana to demonstrate our commitment to protecting all citizens from hate and bigotry. As leaders and representatives of Hoosier citizens, our foremost goal must be to improve our communities. Sometimes, those improvements need to take place as a series of steps in order to build consensus._

_“I believe in choosing progress over total inaction. This is an important and meaningful step toward crafting long-term laws that definitively protect all Hoosiers from crimes committed on the wholly unacceptable basis of hate and intolerance."_

---
|
---
| **State Rep. Greg Steuerwald:** _“The intent of this bill is to cover every form of hate and treat every form of hate the same. This is the most comprehensive bias crimes statute in the country, leaving no one out. With this policy, every Hoosier will receive equal protection under the law, which is a Constitutional right, while reinforcing that bias-motivated crimes in Indiana will not be tolerated.”_

---
|
---
| **Kyle Hupfer, Indiana Republican Party Chairman:** _“This is a day to celebrate. For decades Indiana has tried and failed to enact a sensible and responsible bias crimes bill. But thanks to the steady leadership of Governor Eric Holcomb, who publicly advocated for a bill and privately led negotiations to reach a deal, Indiana is off the list and on the map.

“From the beginning, Governor Holcomb said his goal was to pass a long overdue bias crimes bill to get Indiana off the list of states without one, to reaffirm that Indiana is welcoming to all, and to aid our economic development efforts. This bill achieves all those goals.

“..._ _Leadership is about doing what’s right – and Governor Holcomb is the right leader at the right time.”_

clean_body: Yesterday, Governor Eric Holcomb made history by signing Indiana's first ever bias crimes law. With his signature -- and the the work of member of... | | | | | | | Yesterday, Governor Eric Holcomb made history by signing Indiana's first ever bias crimes law. This new law allows judges to enhance sentences on criminals who commit crimes because of an aggravating bias that targets a group -- better protecting and defending Hoosiers who are victims of senseless crimes. With his leadership -- and the the support of members of the Indiana General Assembly led by House Speaker Brian Bosma and Senate President Pro Tem Rod Bray -- Indiana is now OFF the list of states without a bias crimes law and ON the map of states that have one! Check out what Hoosiers are saying about Indiana's new bias crimes law: Governor Eric Holcomb: “Months ago, I decided to make protecting Hoosiers against bias crimes a key part of my 2019 legislative agenda. Today, finally, Senate Bill 198 accomplished that goal and I will sign it into law as soon as it gets to my desk. “Those targeted for crimes because of color, creed, disability, national origin, race, religion or sexual orientation are protected. But this new law goes further. It also will cover bias crimes committed because of other traits and characteristics, such as gender, or gender identity, or sex, or age, and other commonly targeted groups. “Our new law will allow judges to enhance sentences based on listed and non-listed categories. Criminals who attempt to instill fear by attacking others based, for example, on who someone loves, who they are, how they identify, how they pray, should know their sentences can, and I believe should, be enhanced to the fullest extent of the law.” | | Lt. Governor Suzanne Crouch: "By signing this enrolled act into law, we are widening the umbrella of judicial coverage for more Hoosiers. I stand with Governor Holcomb in the calling of protecting all individuals regardless of their color, creed, disability, national origin, race, religion or sexual orientation and allowing a judge to consider at sentencing whether a crime was committed with bias or because of a victim's real or perceived characteristic, trait, belief, association or other attribute. By adding these descriptions, we are removing Indiana from the list of states that do not have this clarification in statute. I look forward to addressing the needs of our communities so we can continue to take Indiana and the Hoosiers that call it home to the Next Level." | | Speaker Brian Bosma: “This bill provides an excellent solution and it covers all Hoosiers equally. You cannot name a characteristic or trait not covered under this bill. We believe this is the most comprehensive and inclusive bias crimes statute in the nation. The reference to our current bias crime definition meets or exceeds that of 21 other states’ bias crime statutes, and all of those states are off the list of states without a bias crimes law. There’s no reasonable assertion as to why this all-inclusive measure doesn’t take Indiana off the list.” | | Senate President Pro Tem Rodric Bray: “By referencing a specific list of characteristics, Senate Bill 198 provides guidance for judges, while still giving them the flexibility to increase the sentence for any bias-motivated crime as they see fit. I’m pleased to see this bill advance to the governor’s desk. It’s truly inclusive and will provide protections for all Hoosiers.” | | Former Indiana Supreme Court Justice Frank Sullivan, Jr.: “The language of the bill is clear that acts of bias that are specifically referred to are not exclusive: Gender and gender identity, as well as age, citizenship, martial status, etc. are authorized to be used as an aggravating circumstance in determining a sentence every bit as much as race or religion or sexual identity. Just because a characteristic or trait is not specifically listed does not prevent it from being used to impose a harsher sentence. That was the conclusion reach by the Indiana Supreme Court in its unanimous Witmer v. State decision, a case in which I participated while a justice on the court. "...To be clear: If a person thinks that under this law he or she can commit a crime with bias due to a victim's gender or gender identity without risk of a harsher sentence, that person is wrong." | | Kevin Brinegar, Indiana Chamber of Commerce President: “The bias crimes legislation heading to the Governor is a big step in the right direction. Though the list is not as comprehensive as we had advocated for, what the Legislature has passed is still a meaningful hate crimes bill. It is more inclusive than some states’ laws and on par with others. Not to mention, it’s far better than having no law at all. “...No one should want to perpetuate a false perception about our state being unwelcoming; that only exacerbates our workforce challenges.” | | Micah Clark, American Family Association of Indiana Executive Director: “Indiana avoided a California style, left-wing, hate crime bill based on identity politics that could have threatened freedom of speech or provided justice for only a select few. What the legislature passed keeps this issue in the hands of judges, giving them the broadest possible means of sentencing for these crimes.” | | State Senator Jim Merritt, Indianapolis Mayoral Candidate: “Today’s passage of the bias-crimes bill is a substantial first step forward for Indiana to demonstrate our commitment to protecting all citizens from hate and bigotry. As leaders and representatives of Hoosier citizens, our foremost goal must be to improve our communities. Sometimes, those improvements need to take place as a series of steps in order to build consensus. “I believe in choosing progress over total inaction. This is an important and meaningful step toward crafting long-term laws that definitively protect all Hoosiers from crimes committed on the wholly unacceptable basis of hate and intolerance." | | State Rep. Greg Steuerwald: “The intent of this bill is to cover every form of hate and treat every form of hate the same. This is the most comprehensive bias crimes statute in the country, leaving no one out. With this policy, every Hoosier will receive equal protection under the law, which is a Constitutional right, while reinforcing that bias-motivated crimes in Indiana will not be tolerated.” | | Kyle Hupfer, Indiana Republican Party Chairman: “This is a day to celebrate. For decades Indiana has tried and failed to enact a sensible and responsible bias crimes bill. But thanks to the steady leadership of Governor Eric Holcomb, who publicly advocated for a bill and privately led negotiations to reach a deal, Indiana is off the list and on the map. “From the beginning, Governor Holcomb said his goal was to pass a long overdue bias crimes bill to get Indiana off the list of states without one, to reaffirm that Indiana is welcoming to all, and to aid our economic development efforts. This bill achieves all those goals. “... Leadership is about doing what’s right – and Governor Holcomb is the right leader at the right time.” | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Copyright © 2019, All rights reserved. | | |
# **Man Accused of Murdering New Jersey Nanny Was Twice-Deported Illegal Immigrant from Honduras**

“The man charged with murdering a female nanny who went missing while jogging in New Jersey last month was in the U.S. illegally and had twice been deported to Honduras, Immigration and Customs Enforcement confirmed to the _Washington Examiner_ Wednesday,” Anna Giaritelli reports.

“Jorge Rios, 33, has been charged with murdering Carolina Cano, 45, as well as kidnapping and aggravated sexual assault. Both lived in Jersey City, N.J., where the attack took place.” Jersey City designated itself as a sanctuary city in February 2017.

[Click here to read more](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4c913272e5&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|
---
| “Democrats are still reeling from Special Counsel Robert Mueller’s conclusion that the Trump campaign did not collude with Russians in 2016. But they’ve now hit upon a political comeback strategy: Accuse Attorney General William Barr of a coverup,” _[The Wall Street Journal](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=1930b5cc5b&e=3ee4985eea)_ editorial board writes. “That’s preposterous, since Mr. Barr’s four-page letter quotes directly from Mr. Mueller’s report.”
---
|
---
| [KYFL](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=26866f7984&e=3ee4985eea) reports that Louisiana Attorney General Jeff Landry (R-LA) has announced the arrest of an illegal immigrant “accused of more than 100 counts of sex crimes.” The suspect, a registered sex offender, illegally re-entered the United States after being deported and was re-arrested for victimizing a child under 13. “Illegal immigration has real-life consequences,” Attorney General Landry said.
---
|
---
| “According to new research from Zillow, real estate investors appear to be flocking to Opportunity Zones in the U.S., where they can receive a discount on capital gains taxes for investing within certain areas,” Michael Gerrity writes in _[World Property Journal](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=353eeb0e32&e=3ee4985eea)_. Sale prices in Opportunity Zones have grown by more than 20 percent annually, increasing the value of real estate in these historically low-income, distressed communities.
---
|
---
| “A workplace raid in Allen, Texas, by Immigration and Customs Enforcement led to the arrests of 280 workers suspected of illegally working in the United States,” Claude Thompson reports in the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=eef545400d&e=3ee4985eea)_. U.S. law enforcement carried out the operation yesterday in what is being called “the largest workplace raid since before the Obama administration.”
---
|

name: Team Beto
subject: Still reeling
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BETO FOR AMERICA
body:
[1]Beto for America

We’re still reeling from the beautiful wave of people power that swept
across every state and territory on Saturday. Thousands of people joined
the rallies in El Paso, Austin, and Houston, and thousands more gathered
for livestream watch parties for our official launch. Just look:

[1]Tweets from March 30

It was amazing to see. It gives us real leverage to build the largest and
most inclusive grassroots campaign for president in history. But the next
step is even more important. It’s time to start talking to voters in our
communities.

Knocking on doors and talking face-to-face with voters (also known as
canvassing) is proven as the single most effective way to win votes. To
get enough votes to win, it’ll take thousands of people across the country
going door-to-door.

So April 13-14, we’re organizing a national weekend of canvasses. We’re
asking you to step up as a
leader and host one in your community.

Hosting a canvass is easy. It means printing materials, meeting and
training volunteers, and sending back the data collected. We’ll provide
all of the support and materials you need, and we’ll help you recruit for
your event.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/signup-canvass-host/?source=ema20190404&t=1001&akid=26495%2E444211%2EIRzJO6 ]Are you ready? Sign up to host a canvass here, pick an outdoor meeting
place and time, and fill out your event details. We’ll follow up with
everything you need.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/signup-canvass-host/?source=ema20190404&t=1002&akid=26495%2E444211%2EIRzJO6 ]Sign up to host a canvass »

We’ll never be able to hire enough staff in enough places to get to the
scale required to win and move millions of voters. So Beto is counting on
volunteers like you taking the leap and hosting canvasses. To reach all of
those voters, we have to start now.

Thank you for believing in this campaign,

Team Beto

Contribute to Beto's campaign: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/beto-for-america-emails?refcode=em20190404footer&t=1003&refcode2=26495_444211_IRzJO6&akid=26495%2E444211%2EIRzJO6

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to act. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1004&akid=26495%2E444211%2EIRzJO6

PAID FOR BY BETO FOR AMERICA
P.O. Box 3628 El Paso, TX 79923

subject: Your healthcare

body:
You've got to give Donald Trump and Mitch McConnell an A for effort
|
---
[ ](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa2f0/18d6a294/4af091d1/26d6aa77/1966794613/VEsH/ "https://www.jamesmackler.com/")

| | You've got to give Donald Trump and Mitch McConnell an A for effort, honestly. Even after more than seventy failed attempts, they're still trying to scrap the Affordable Care Act and take away health coverage for millions of Americans. While that bill isn't perfect -- we need substantive fixes to keep our hospitals open, reduce costs, expand coverage, and tackle prescription drug prices -- instead of fixing it, Trump is now calling on the courts to **strike down the entire law.** This move is nothing less than an attack on all Tennesseans. **More than one million in our state could be denied coverage** due to a preexisting condition if the president gets his way, in large part because our last governor wouldn't (or couldn't) expand Medicaid in Tennessee. [**_I want to hear from you: how has the ACA impacted you? Do you have a pre-existing condition? What would repealing the ACA mean to you and your family? Let me know >>_**](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa2f0/18d6a294/4af091d1/26d6aa74/1966794613/VEsE/ "https://secure.jamesmackler.com/e190404") | | | [TAKE THE SURVEY >>](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa2f0/18d6a294/4af091d1/26d6aa74/1966794613/VEsF/ "https://secure.jamesmackler.com/e190404")
---
Nothing gets done when Washington politicians stick to their hyper-partisan political calculations, and it's hard working folks across Tennessee who pay the price.
**Enough is enough.** No more sabotage, no more partisanship -- it's time for real solutions.
[**_Tell me about your experiences with the ACA here. I look forward to reading your responses._**](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa2f0/18d6a294/4af091d1/26d6aa74/1966794613/VEsC/ "https://secure.jamesmackler.com/e190404")
Thank you,
James

name: Kamala HQ
subject: A quick survey
body:
Yesterday,
Kamala sent a survey to our top supporters asking for their input to help
guide our campaign in this next phase.

The results from this survey will guide our strategy as Kamala hits the
campaign trail in key states and prepares for the first primary debate in
June.

[ https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/kdh-petition-190402-april-survey?firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&refcode=ema190404-full&source=ema190404-full&t=1001&akid=4222%2E5755789%2EWeVpZq ]Our records show you haven’t taken Kamala’s April survey yet. Can you
take a few moments today to make your voice heard on the issues that
matter most?

[ https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/kdh-petition-190402-april-survey?firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&refcode=ema190404-full&source=ema190404-full&t=1002&akid=4222%2E5755789%2EWeVpZq ]Take the survey

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

Original message from Kamala Harris, April 3, 2019

I’m reaching out to you now because I need your input as our campaign
enters its next phase.

The coming months will be critical. We’re making big investments in
grassroots organizing to build this campaign from the ground up. I will be
on the campaign trail in key states to keep sharing our positive message
and platform with voters. And in June, I’ll be taking the debate stage to
share more of our ideas with the nation and contrast our campaign with
others in the race.

As my team and I work to put together our strategy for the coming months,
I want to make sure your voice is heard.

[ https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/kdh-petition-190402-april-survey?firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&refcode=ema190404-full&source=ema190404-full&t=1003&akid=4222%2E5755789%2EWeVpZq ]Can you complete our short April survey to share your input with me?
Among other things, I want to hear what you think about the challenges we
face, what’s driving your decision in this election, and what has you
excited about 2020.

[ https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/kdh-petition-190402-april-survey?firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&refcode=ema190404-full&source=ema190404-full&t=1004&akid=4222%2E5755789%2EWeVpZq ]Take the survey

We are running a campaign we can all be proud of -- because it’s about
people. Ours is truly a campaign of, by and for the people. All the hard
work we are doing to win this election is personal.

That’s why it’s so important that my team and I hear directly from you
about the issues that matter to you, your family, and your community.
Online surveys like this one are tremendously helpful -- they let me and
my team know what’s on your mind.

[ https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/kdh-petition-190402-april-survey?firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&refcode=ema190404-full&source=ema190404-full&t=1005&akid=4222%2E5755789%2EWeVpZq ]So please, if you can, take a few minutes to fill out our April survey.

[ https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/kdh-petition-190402-april-survey?firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&refcode=ema190404-full&source=ema190404-full&t=1006&akid=4222%2E5755789%2EWeVpZq ]Take the survey

Thanks for taking the time to share your thoughts with me.

For The People,

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: President Trump: ‘No citizen will be forgotten’
body:
The day he took office, President Donald J. Trump made a promise to the American people. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0f483ee2c3&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • April 4, 2019
---
| | |
# **President Trump: ‘No citizen will be forgotten’**

**The day he took office,** President Donald J. Trump made a promise to the American people. “The forgotten men and women of our country will be forgotten no longer. Everyone is listening to you now.”

**For years** , our government didn’t operate that way. Washington put its own interests and grand plans above the basic, everyday needs of our citizens. Outside the Beltway, “a different reality exists,” President Trump said on Inauguration Day—children trapped in poverty in our inner cities, rusted-out factories scattered across our heartland, and crime and drugs rooted in too many of our communities.

**Today, President Trump** took another major step toward reversing that decline and restoring the American Dream for all.

**Speaking from the Cabinet Room,** the President kicked off the inaugural meeting of the White House Opportunity and Revitalization Council, which will lead the Administration’s effort to renew hope and dignity in distressed parts of our country. Scott Turner, a former NFL player and state legislator, will oversee the Council’s work.

**“This is very close to my heart,”** Turner said.

**Fifty-two million Americans** live in economically distressed communities. Despite the historic streak of job creation and wage growth under President Trump, these areas are often plagued by high poverty levels, failing schools, and a scarcity of jobs. To reverse their fortunes, the 2017 Tax Cuts and Jobs Act created massive incentives for investment in these places, creating jobs and opportunities where they are needed the most.

**The early returns** are incredible in these “Opportunity Zones,” which cover 8,700 communities across all 50 states. Property sale prices have [soared more than 20 percent](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=357430cc5a&e=3ee4985eea) annually in these zones, building wealth for the people who live there and encouraging more economic activity in the process.

**The new Council** , under Turner’s leadership, will build on this success. “Our actions will directly improve the lives of countless low-income Americans,” President Trump said today. “Together we can lift up every forgotten community.”

[The Trump tax cut boom, by the numbers](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=191ca2c271&e=3ee4985eea)

**Watch:** [Meet Scott Turner, who will lead the Opportunity Zone charge](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a6838dd86d&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **Video of the day: Smugglers push children through barbed wire barrier **
---
| [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d16bc4fc23&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **On December 18,** U.S. Border Patrol captured footage of human smugglers pushing family units through an old, barbed-wire section of border fencing near Yuma, Arizona. The image of children being forced along in this journey speaks louder than any words can.

**This is the crisis** Congressional Democrats and their media allies don’t want you to see—and the one President Trump is fighting to end.

clean_body: The day he took office, President Donald J. Trump made a promise to the American people. | | | | | The White House • April 4, 2019 | | | President Trump: ‘No citizen will be forgotten’ The day he took office, President Donald J. Trump made a promise to the American people. “The forgotten men and women of our country will be forgotten no longer. Everyone is listening to you now.” For years , our government didn’t operate that way. Washington put its own interests and grand plans above the basic, everyday needs of our citizens. Outside the Beltway, “a different reality exists,” President Trump said on Inauguration Day—children trapped in poverty in our inner cities, rusted-out factories scattered across our heartland, and crime and drugs rooted in too many of our communities. Today, President Trump took another major step toward reversing that decline and restoring the American Dream for all. Speaking from the Cabinet Room, the President kicked off the inaugural meeting of the White House Opportunity and Revitalization Council, which will lead the Administration’s effort to renew hope and dignity in distressed parts of our country. Scott Turner, a former NFL player and state legislator, will oversee the Council’s work. “This is very close to my heart,” Turner said. Fifty-two million Americans live in economically distressed communities. Despite the historic streak of job creation and wage growth under President Trump, these areas are often plagued by high poverty levels, failing schools, and a scarcity of jobs. To reverse their fortunes, the 2017 Tax Cuts and Jobs Act created massive incentives for investment in these places, creating jobs and opportunities where they are needed the most. The early returns are incredible in these “Opportunity Zones,” which cover 8,700 communities across all 50 states. Property sale prices have annually in these zones, building wealth for the people who live there and encouraging more economic activity in the process. The new Council , under Turner’s leadership, will build on this success. “Our actions will directly improve the lives of countless low-income Americans,” President Trump said today. “Together we can lift up every forgotten community.” Watch: | | Video of the day: Smugglers push children through barbed wire barrier | | On December 18, U.S. Border Patrol captured footage of human smugglers pushing family units through an old, barbed-wire section of border fencing near Yuma, Arizona. The image of children being forced along in this journey speaks louder than any words can. This is the crisis Congressional Democrats and their media allies don’t want you to see—and the one President Trump is fighting to end. | | | | | | The White House 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW Washington, DC 20500 * USA · 202-456-1111

name: Kirsten Gillibrand
subject: One person, one vote
body:
We need to restore the principle our democracy is built on.
[](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f976dec/2ae112ef/6cd86c68/357db0b5/3209435015/VEsH/ "https://2020.kirstengillibrand.com/")
[](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f976dec/2ae112ef/6cd86c68/357db0b5/3209435015/VEsE/ "https://2020.kirstengillibrand.com/")
Peter, Donald Trump lost the popular vote by nearly 3 million votes--but he is the one in the Oval Office.
When the results of our elections don't reflect the will of the American people--when the power of your vote depends on the state you live in--something is seriously wrong in our democracy.
**We need to fix that. We need to end the Electoral College.**
I'm fighting to get rid of this broken system once and for all. The principle of one person, one vote is foundational to our democracy. It can't function until we restore that principle.
**Each of us needs to be bold enough to give this fight everything we 've got. We need 5,000 people to speak out before midnight demanding an end to the Electoral College, and I need you to be one of them:**
**[Add your name to demand an end to the Electoral College to restore the principle of one person, one vote and ensure our elections always reflect the will of our country.](https://secure.kirstengillibrand. "https://action.kirstengillibrand.com/kg-electoralcollege?firstName=Peter&lastName=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk3008-ja-gfp-nd&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fact.kirstengillibrand.com%2Fkg-electoralcollege")**
This is a fight for the future of our democracy, and the stakes are simply too high to stay silent.
Thank you for taking a brave stand for our democracy,
Kirsten Gillibrand
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
